title: In My Bedroom
description: In My Bedroom is a book by Donna Hill. The exported reason says: Threat to good order/security of institution as it contains material describing or depicting sexual acts involving children
descriptionParagraphs: In My Bedroom is a book by Donna Hill. The exported reason says: Threat to good order/security of institution as it contains material describing or depicting sexual acts involving children

Its interest lies partly in the way literary or informational writing gets collapsed into a public-morality problem. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Michigan. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: The Michigan prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: Threat to good. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: In My Bedroom is a book by Donna Hill. The exported reason says: Threat to good order/security of institution as it contains material describing or depicting sexual acts involving children
banningParagraphs: In My Bedroom entered censorship debates as a book associated with morality, print scandal, and sexuality. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around instructional harm, obscenity, public morality, and public order.

The earliest event currently captured here is 2004-05-01 in Michigan, where Michigan corrections agencies and prison mailrooms excluded from prison circulation. The Michigan prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: Threat to good. The Michigan prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: Threat to good order/security of institution as it.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: Literature Suppressed on Sexual Grounds
counterReadings.author: Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: Surveys the legal and moral language used to suppress books as obscene.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
